resources.string:
This software will run games for the Nintendo 3DS handheld game console. No game titles are included.

Before you can begin with emulating, please select a folder to store CitraVR's user data in.

What's this:
Wiki - CitraVR Android user data and storage

Next, you will need to select a Game Folder. CitraVR will display all of the 3DS ROMs inside of the selected folder in the app.\n\nCIA ROMs, updates and DLC will need to be installed separately by clicking on the folder icon and selecting Install CIA.
Share CitraVR\'s log file to debug issues
Install alternative drivers for potentially better performance or accuracy
Custom driver loading isn\'t currently supported for this device.\nCheck this option again in the future to see if support was added!
Allows CitraVR to populate the games list
Changes the files that CitraVR uses to load games
Modify the look of the app
Would you like to replace your current GPU driver?
Invalid driver selected, using system default!
Made with \u2764 from the CitraVR team
Projects that make CitraVR for Android possible
Learn how to setup CitraVR and jump into emulation.
Select your Games folder with the button below.
Grant the notification permission with the button below.
CitraVR won\'t be able to notify you of important information.
Azahar requires permission to manage files on this device in order to store and manage its data.\n\nPlease grant the \"Filesystem\" permission before continuing.
Grant the camera permission below to emulate the 3DS camera.
Grant the microphone permission below to emulate the 3DS microphone.
Grant the filesystem permission below to allow Azahar to store files.
Games won\'t be displayed in the Games list if a folder isn\'t selected.

Select data folders
(User folder is required)
Grant optional permissions to use specific features of the emulator
Select your user data directory with the button below.

You appear to have user directories set for both Lime3DS and Azahar. This is likely because you upgraded to Azahar, and when prompted chose a different user directory than what was being used for Lime3DS.

This may have resulted in you thinking you lost saves or other settings - we apologize if that happened.

Would you like to go back to using your original Lime3DS user directory, restoring settings and save games from Lime3DS, or keep your current Azahar user directory?

Neither directory will be deleted, regardless of your choice, and you may freely switch between them using the Select User Folder option.
This step is required to allow CitraVR to work. Please select a directory and then you can continue.

You have lost write permissions on your user data directory, where saves and other information are stored. This can happen after some app or Android updates. Please re-select the directory to regain permissions so you can continue.
The user directory selection was invalid.\nPlease re-select the user directory, ensuring that you navigate to it from the root of your device\'s storage.
Azahar has lost permission to manage files on this device. This can happen after some app or Android updates. Please re-grant this permission on the next screen to continue using the app.
Configure your theme preferences for Azahar.
Apply standard gamepad mapping for all buttons and axes
Press this button on your controller!
3DS face button diamond with A button highlighted
This will remove all current controller bindings.
If the "Hotkey Enable" key is mapped, that key must be pressed in addition to the mapped hotkey
Some controllers may not be able to map their D-pad as an axis. If that\'s the case, use the D-Pad (buttons) section.
Only map the D-pad to these if you\'re facing issues with the D-Pad (Axis) button mappings.
Press or move an input to bind it to %1$s.
Move your joystick up or down.
Move your joystick left or right.
This control must be bound to a gamepad analog stick or D-pad axis!
This control must be bound to a gamepad button!
Perform system file operations such as installing system files or booting the Home Menu

Azahar needs console unique data and firmware files from a real console to be able to use some of its features. Such files and data can be set up with the Azahar Artic Setup Tool.
Notes:
  • This operation will install console unique data to Azahar, do not share your user or nand folders after performing the setup process!
  • While doing the setup process, Azahar will link to the console running the setup tool. You can unlink the console later from the System Files tab in the emulator options menu.
  • Do not go online with both Azahar and your 3DS console at the same time after setting up system files, as this could cause issues.
  • Old 3DS setup is needed for the New 3DS setup to work (setting up both is recommended).
  • Both setup modes will work regardless of the model of the console running the setup tool.
Fetching current system files status, please wait...

This action will unlink your real console from Azahar, with the following consequences:
  • Your OTP, SecureInfo and LocalFriendCodeSeed will be removed from Azahar.
  • Your friend list will reset and you will be logged out of your NNID/PNID account.
  • System files and eshop titles obtained through Azahar will become inaccessible until the same console is linked again using the setup tool (save data will not be lost).

Continue?
Show HOME menu apps in Applications list
Run System Setup when the HOME Menu is launched
Apply region free patch to installed applications
Patches the region of installed applications to be region free, so that they always appear on the home menu.
Enable required LLE modules for online features (if installed)
Enables the LLE modules required for online multiplayer, eShop access, etc.
Set the emulated 3DS clock to either reflect that of your device or start at a simulated date and time.
If the clock is set to \"Simulated clock\", this changes the fixed date and time to start at.
Number of steps per hour reported by the pedometer. Range from 0 to 65,535.

This will replace your current virtual 3DS with a new one. Your current virtual 3DS will not be recoverable. This might have unexpected effects in games. This might fail if you use an outdated config savegame. Continue?
This will replace your current MAC address with a new one. It is not recommended to do this if you got the MAC address from your real console using the setup tool. Continue?
Loads 3GX plugins from the emulated SD card if they are available.
Allow Games to Change Plugin Loader State
Allows homebrew apps to enable the plugin loader even when it is disabled.
The country setting is not valid for the selected emulated region.
The country setting is not valid for the current linked console.
Compresses the content of CIA files when installed to the emulated SD card. Only affects CIA content which is installed while the setting is enabled.
Sets the image source of the virtual camera. You can use an image file, or a device camera when supported.
If the \"Image Source\" setting is set to \"Device Camera\", this sets the physical camera to use.
Emits the fragment shader used to emulate PICA using SPIR-V instead of GLSL
Disables the SPIR-V optimization pass, reducing stuttering considerably while barely affecting performance.
Compiles shaders in the background to reduce stuttering during gameplay. When enabled expect temporary graphical glitches
Enables linear filtering, which causes game visuals to appear smoother.
Scales the screens with an integer multiplier of the original 3DS screen. For layouts with two different screen sizes, the largest screen is integer-scaled.
Enhances the visuals of games by applying a filter to textures. The supported filters are Anime4K Ultrafast, Bicubic, ScaleForce, and xBRZ freescale.
Delay the game render thread when it submits data to the GPU. Helps with performance issues in the (very few) applications with dynamic framerates.
Overrides the sampling filter used by games. This can be useful in certain cases with poorly behaved games when upscaling. If unsure, set this to Game Controlled.
Uses more accurate multiplication in hardware shaders, which may fix some graphical bugs. When enabled, performance will be reduced.
Uses a separate thread to emulate the GPU asynchronously. When enabled, performance will be improved.
When enabled, emulation speed will be limited to a specified percentage of normal speed.
Specifies the percentage to limit emulation speed. With the default of 100% emulation will be limited to normal speed. Values higher or lower will increase or decrease the speed limit.
Prevent 3DS camera, screenshot, and custom texture images from being indexed by Android and displayed in the gallery. Your device may need to be rebooted after changing this setting to take effect.
Emulation speed limit used while the turbo hotkey is active.
Expands the display area to include the cutout (or notch) area.
Specifies the resolution used to render at. A high resolution will improve visual quality a lot but is also quite heavy on performance and might cause glitches in certain games.
Turning off this setting will significantly reduce emulation performance! For the best experience, it is recommended that you leave this setting enabled.
Warning: Modifying these settings will slow emulation
Choose the stereoscopic 3D mode for 3D rendering. Side by Side modes are most common in modern use. Anaglyph and Interlaced modes will always apply to all connected displays.
Specifies the value of the 3D slider. This should be set to higher than 0% when Stereoscopic 3D is enabled.
Greatly improves performance in some applications, but can cause flickering in others.
Swaps which eye is shown in which side. Combined with Side by Side mode makes it possible to see 3D by crossing your eyes!
Whether to enable stereoscopic 3D, and on which displays. The single display options are only relevant when multiple displays are connected.
Scales the screen to a percentage of its original size.
Specifies the percentage of empty space to shift the screens horizontally. Positive values move the two eyes closer to the middle, while negative values move them away.
Specifies the percentage of empty space to shift the screens vertically. Positive values move the two eyes towards the bottom, while negative values move them towards the top.
Reduce stuttering by storing and loading generated shaders to disk. It cannot be used without Enabling Hardware Shader.
Textures are dumped to dump/textures/[Title ID]/.
Textures are loaded from load/textures/[Title ID]/.
Loads all custom textures into memory. This feature can use a lot of memory.
Load custom textures asynchronously with background threads to reduce loading stutter.
Stretches audio to reduce stuttering. When enabled, increases audio latency and slightly reduces performance.
Scales audio playback speed to account for drops in emulation framerate. This means that audio will play at full speed even while the game framerate is low. May cause audio desync issues.
Uses the Just-in-Time (JIT) compiler for CPU emulation. When enabled, game performance will be significantly improved.
Uses hardware to emulate 3DS shaders. When enabled, game performance will be significantly improved.
Synchronizes the game frame rate to the refresh rate of your device.
Log additional graphics related debug information. When enabled, game performance will be significantly reduced.
Flush Log Output on Every Message
Immediately commits the debug log to file. Use this if Azahar crashes and the log output is being cut.
Delays the start of the app when LLE modules are enabled.
Makes async operations deterministic for debugging. Enabling this may cause freezes.
Enables the RPC server on port 45987. This allows remotely reading/writing guest memory.
Toggles the unique data console type (Old 3DS ↔ New 3DS) to be able to download the opposite system firmware type from system settings.
Use the JIT engine instead of the interpreter for software shader emulation.
Which layouts are cycled by the Cycle Layout hotkey
Please follow the guides to redump your game cartidges or installed titles.
All advanced settings will be reset to their default configuration. This can not be undone.
Do you want to reset this setting back to its default value?
This setting is currently disabled due to another setting not being the appropriate value.
This option can\'t be changed while a game is running.
The game properties could not be loaded.
Press Back to access the menu.
Hold original and currently pressed button
The layout used by a connected secondary screen, wired or wireless (Chromecast, Miracast)
The color which appears behind the screens during emulation, represented as an RGB value.
The opacity of the second 3DS screen when using a custom screen layout. Useful if the second screen is to be positioned on top of the first screen.
Where should the small screen appear relative to the large one in Large Screen Layout?
Gap between screens in all two-screen modes. Measured in px relative to the 240px height of the larger screen.
How many times larger is the large screen than the small screen in Large Screen layout?
Use Artic Controller when connected to Artic Base Server
Use the controls provided by Artic Base Server when connected to it instead of the configured input device.
Are you sure that you would like to close the current game?
While loading the specified Amiibo file, an error occurred. Please check that the file is correct.
You need to allow write access to external storage for the emulator to work
The external storage needs to be available in order to use CitraVR
No files were found or no game directory has been selected yet.
Warning: Savestates are NOT a replacement for in-game saves, and are not meant to be reliable.\n\nUse at your own risk!
Text length is not correct (should be %d characters)
Text is too long (should be no more than %d characters)
CitraVR needs to access your camera to emulate the 3DS\'s cameras.\n\nAlternatively, you can also set \"Image Source\" to \"Still Image\" in Camera Settings.
CitraVR needs to access your microphone to emulate the 3DS\'s microphone.\n\nAlternatively, you can also change \"Audio Input Device\" in Audio Settings.
%s is missing. Please dump your system archives.\nContinuing emulation may result in crashes and bugs.
Installation Failed. CIA file not found.
A fatal error occurred. Check the log for details.\nContinuing emulation may result in crashes and bugs.
New 3DS exclusive applications cannot be loaded without enabling the New 3DS mode.
Select which graphics API to delete the shader cache
Deleting shader cache for title, please wait…
Configure whether the performance overlay is shown and what information is displayed.
Display the amount of RAM getting used by the emulator.
Display the amount of RAM which is available.
Display current Battery temperature in Celsius and Fahrenheit.
Choose where the performance overlay is displayed on the screen.
Adds a background behind the overlay for easier reading.
Are you sure you want to delete \"%1$s\"?

plurals.name: cia_install_toast
plurals.item: Installing %d file. See notification for more details.
Installing %d files. See notification for more details.

resources.string: The installation of \"%s\" was aborted.\n Please see the log for more details
\"%s\" must be decrypted before being used with CitraVR.\n A real 3DS is required
An unknown error occurred while installing \"%s\".\n Please see the log for more details
Use system colors across the app
Change the color theme of the app\'s menus
When using the dark theme, apply black backgrounds.
Failed to communicate with the Artic Base server. Emulation will stop.
Connect to a real console that is running an Artic Base server
Compression not supported for this file.
Decompression not supported for this file.
Already installed applications cannot be compressed or decompressed.
If the clock is set to \"Simulated clock\", this changes the fixed date and time to start at.
Detected a fatal launch bug with CitraVR. This is a known issue. \n\nThe problem is now resolved. Please try launching the game again.
Higher levels may improve audio/emulation performance, but will drain the battery faster
Extends the top screen around a cylinder for immersive gameplay
DANGER: not all games/levels look good in immersive mode. Large visual artifacts are common when this option is selected
WARNING: this WILL cause visual artifacting in most content
Should be enabled for most titles. When disabled, improves performance of specific titles (e. g. Star Fox 64 3D)
Projects that make Citra and CitraVR possible

Overrides the value of other settings to minimize overhead and maxmimize performance. NOTE: when this setting is enabled, the user\'s values for the following settings are ignored: VR Environment (always selects Void), Audio stretching (disabled)

During your first playthrough of a game or level, you might experience brief pauses or audio stutters. \n\nAfter the shaders are loaded, they are stored to the device, preventing these stutters on subsequent launches.
Some games just won\'t run well. Be sure to check the CitraVR Game Compatibility List to see which games perform best.
\nHaving trouble? Press Select + Start to disable Gamepad Gaze Mode, then try again
[default: -1 = use auto-detect] CAUTION: Sets the register offset used for the view when using Super Immersive Mode Profile 2
[Leave blank for auto-detect] CAUTION: Sets the register and index offset used for identifying which eye is being drawn e.g 87,2
Adjusts how much the depth slider affects the game camera (movement/separation), some games require smaller/larger values, so this multiplier adjusts how much movement the depth setting causes
Press and hold the index trigger while targeting the panel.
Slide the motion controller up or down to adjust the panel\'s vertical position.
(Top Panel) Move the thumbstick forward to extend deeper or pull backward to bring it closer.
(Lower Panel) Move the thumbstick to adjust the panel angle towards or away from you.
Use the toggle to enable or disable horizontal movement.
With horizontal movement enabled, slide left or right to adjust horizontally.
Press this button to reset position.
